coupling.rationale: Fixes are well-contained within their packages but Octokit service location (packages/opencode/src/github/) creates dual-mode ambiguity — it must work both via direct Octokit (CLI) and via IPC proxy (desktop renderer). This isn't a hard coupling problem, but the missing seam design means the implementation will likely leak IPC concerns into the effect service. Fix-B and fix-E modify the same 3 files — not a coupling issue per se but creates merge contention. DesktopMenuAction union type change in desktop-menu.ts propagates to 9 consumer files across 2 packages.

debuggability.rationale: Effect service pattern (Schema.TaggedError) provides good typed error paths for fix-C. However, the IPC proxy (fix-E) uses bare ipcMain.handle with throw-on-error — no structured error types. Token store failures (fix-B) use generic Error with string message, not typed. The deep-link OAuth callback will yield an opaque 'opencode://github-oauth?code=...' string — if the code exchange fails, no typed GitHubOAuthError is specified. DesktopMenuAction handler for github.createPullRequest will need to flow through the existing switch that has no error handling.
concerns: OAuth code exchange has no defined error type
DesktopMenuAction handler has no error return

convergence.rationale: Strong convergence. Fix-A revives existing dead code (parseGitHubRemote) and aligns with existing Reference type patterns. Fix-B follows established RESERVED_STORE_NAMES pattern from MCP implementation. Fix-C uses existing Effect service pattern (matching repository-cache.ts style). Fix-D and Fix-E follow existing IPC bridge patterns. The only deviation is adding UI to review-tab.tsx vs extracting into a dedicated component — the existing component is 172 lines of pure diff rendering, and adding PR state management inline would diverge from single-responsibility design.
concerns: review-tab.tsx PR UI should be extracted to separate component, not inlined

surface_area.rationale: Changes touch 18 files across 3 packages. New exports: parseGitHubRemote exposed from repository.ts, new github/* module exports from packages/opencode/src/index.ts, new ElectronAPI methods in preload/types.ts, new DesktopMenuAction variant. The DesktopMenuAction union type change affects all 9 consumer files — any package outside this scope that uses the type will break. i18n scope is incomplete (only en.ts, 15 locale files unmentioned). The plan rightly identifies the need for produce_fragment on 5 shared files to manage contention.
concerns: DesktopMenuAction union change breaks all consumers
i18n missing from 15 non-English locale files

testability.rationale: Fix-A is inherently testable (fixing broken test + adding new test cases). Fix-C can be unit-tested with mock Octokit in 10-line bun -e scripts. Fix-D (UI) lacks SolidJS component test infrastructure but the component is simple enough for manual verification. Fix-B and Fix-E require manual testing (OAuth browser flow, deep-link callback) which is expected for Electron features. The test plan is clear and incremental. Missing: no test for IPC proxy URL validation.
concerns: No test for IPC proxy URL allowlist validation
UI component (fix-D) has no test coverage specified

error_clarity.rationale: Weakest axis. OAuth flow errors: if the user denies GitHub authorization, the deep-link callback receives an error parameter (?error=access_denied) but no error type is defined for this. Token exchange: if POST to github.com/login/oauth/access_token fails, the plan doesn't specify error propagation. IPC proxy errors: throw new Error('Access denied') doesn't identify itself as a GitHub API proxy error. DesktopMenuAction: the switch has no default case — unknown actions silently do nothing. The plan defines no GitHub-specific error types despite creating an entire service layer.
concerns: No GitHubError defined in plan (only Schema.TaggedErrorClass for repository errors)
IPC proxy errors are generic Error with no typed discriminator
DesktopMenuAction switch has no error/unknown case

reversibility.rationale: The plan has a clear reverse-order rollback. Fixes are sequential (each depends on previous) but the dependency graph is well-documented in fix-dependencies. The only concern is fix-B and fix-E modifying the same 3 files — reverting one without the other would require partial reverts of ipc.ts, preload/types.ts, and preload/index.ts. However, the changes are additive (new IPC handlers, new bridge methods), so reverting by removing the git changes is clean.
concerns: fix-B and fix-E share 3 files — partial revert requires careful file-level undo

blocking_findings.finding: Token stored in plaintext via electron-store. No safeStorage usage. GitHub token with repo write access stored as plain JSON at app.getPath('userData').
blocking_findings.repair: Use electron safeStorage.encryptString() on write, safeStorage.decryptString() on read. Or document explicit v1 trade-off and file follow-up issue.

blocking_findings.finding: OAuth flow is underspecified. Plan mentions PKCE vs Device Flow but chooses neither. PKCE requires `opencode://github-oauth` deep-link handler in deep-links.ts (new parsing function). Device Flow needs different UX. File not listed as affected.
blocking_findings.repair: Choose PKCE. Add parseGitHubOAuthCallback to deep-links.ts. Add github-oauth hostname routing in renderer deep-link listener. Add deep-links.ts to affected files list.

blocking_findings.finding: IPC proxy URL validation is mentioned as risk but no concrete allowlist specified. Renderer could potentially make arbitrary HTTP requests from main process with stored token.
blocking_findings.repair: Specify URL validation: parse URL, extract hostname, match against ['api.github.com', 'uploads.github.com'] allowlist. Reject with typed GitHubProxyError if no match.

non_blocking_findings.finding: Octokit service dual-mode design unspecified. Service in packages/opencode must work via direct Octokit (CLI) AND via IPC proxy (desktop renderer). Plan doesn't specify the seam.
non_blocking_findings.repair: Define GitHubService as Effect interface with two implementations: DirectOctokit and IpcProxy. Wire via Layer.choice or Layer.provideMerge.

non_blocking_findings.finding: produce_fragment for DesktopMenuAction union type is fragile — type definition and menu constant are both in desktop-menu.ts but fragments for the same file may overlap.
non_blocking_findings.repair: Consolidate DesktopMenuAction type edit + DESKTOP_MENU constant edit into a single batch edit or fragment for packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts.

non_blocking_findings.finding: No GitHub-specific error types defined. OAuth callback errors, token exchange failures, and IPC proxy errors use generic Error.
non_blocking_findings.repair: Define GitHubOAuthError, GitHubApiError, GitHubProxyError as Schema.TaggedErrorClass. Add to error union in types.ts.

conditions_for_approval: Must resolve F1: token encryption via safeStorage or explicit v1 trade-off issue
Must resolve F2: choose OAuth flow (recommend PKCE) and add deep-link handler in deep-links.ts
Must resolve F3: specify IPC proxy URL allowlist and validation function
Must add deep-links.ts to affected files list (was missing from plan)
follow_up_issues: Add i18n github.* keys to remaining 15 locale files (currently only en.ts listed)
Add SolidJS component tests for GitHub PR creation UI (fix-D component extraction)
CI/CD status polling strategy — plan defines getPullRequestStatus but no polling interval or rate-limit handling
safeStorage migration — if F1 is deferred, file follow-up: migrate github-auth store to encrypted storage
summary: Well-structured plan with clear dependency ordering and rollback strategy. Three blocking findings all relate to security and architectural underspecification: plaintext token storage (safeStorage), unresolved OAuth flow choice (PKCE missing deep-link handler), and unspecified IPC proxy URL validation. Non-blocking findings about dual-mode Octokit service design and missing error types. Recommend revising to fix blocking issues, then resubmit for re-review.
